revision.comment: Tenrikyo wiki website (original article, not forked from Wikipedia)
revision.text:
{{Short description|Online wiki about Tenrikyo}} {{Infobox website | name = Tenrikyo Resource Wiki | logo = | screenshot = | caption = | url = http://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/ | type = Online encyclopedia | language = English | owner = Lewis Nakao | launch date = active since 2011–2012 | current status = Online }}
The '''Tenrikyo Resource Wiki''' (often abbreviated '''TRW''') is a community-edited online encyclopedia focusing on the Japanese religion [[Tenrikyo]]. It describes itself as an initiative “to centralize all information on Tenrikyo and its teachings” and to serve as a one-stop reference for doctrine, history, scriptures, and practice.<ref>{{cite web |title=TRW:About |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/TRW%3AAbout |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/ |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> Independent sites and blogs describe TRW as an unofficial resource maintained by volunteers associated with the “Tenrikyo Resource” project.<ref>{{cite web |title=What is Tenrikyo? |url=https://thedailyofudesaki.wordpress.com/what-is-tenrikyo/ |website=The Daily Ofudesaki |date=2017 |quote=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki (unofficial). |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Links |url=https://tenrikyology.com/links/ |website=Tenrikyology |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==History== Evidence of the wiki’s activity dates to 2011–2012. A Wikimedia Commons file uploaded in September 2011 cites a TRW file page as its source, indicating the English-language subdomain was already operating,<ref>{{cite web |title=File:Tenrikyo Kanrodai sketch.jpg |url=https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tenrikyo_Kanrodai_sketch.jpg |website=Wikimedia Commons |date=September 22, 2011 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> and a 2012 “Weekly Wiki” blog post highlighted TRW pages to introduce Tenrikyo topics to readers.<ref>{{cite web |title=What is Tenrikyo? – Weekly Wiki |url=https://tenrikyo-resource.com/2448/what-is-tenrikyo-weekly-wiki?wptouch_switch=mobile |website=Tenrikyo Resource |date=October 12, 2012 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> During the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami, a Tenrikyo organization’s site directed readers to the project’s earlier domain (wiki.tenrikyo-resource.com) for updated relief information, suggesting the wiki’s use as a community bulletin and reference at the time.<ref>{{cite web |title=Japan Earthquake Relief Fund |url=https://www.tenri.org/general/japan_fund.shtml |website=Tenrikyo Mission Headquarters in America |date=2011 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==Content and features== TRW hosts articles on core Tenrikyo concepts, scriptures, and practices, including entries on the ''[[Ofudesaki]]'' (“Tip of the Writing Brush”), the [[Mikagura-uta|''Kagura'' Service]] (''Tsutome''), doctrinal terms such as “Truth of Origin” (''moto no ri''), and roles such as ''yoboku'' (missionary).<ref>{{cite web |title=Ofudesaki |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Ofudesaki |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Kagura Service |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Kagura_Service |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Truth of Origin |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Truth_of_Origin |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Yoboku |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Yoboku |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> Additional sections include a downloads hub for reference materials and a glossary that aggregates terminology from “Tenrikyo Online.”<ref>{{cite web |title=Downloads |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Downloads |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Glossary Online |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Glossary_Online |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==Organization and contributors== The wiki is part of a broader “Tenrikyo Resource” presence that shares news and materials via social media and external sites; a public Facebook group describes its aim as providing “up-to-date news, articles, and information” related to Tenrikyo.<ref>{{cite web |title=Tenrikyo Resource (Facebook group) |url=https://www.facebook.com/groups/2202874374/ |website=Facebook |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Links |url=https://tenrikyology.com/links/ |website=Tenrikyology |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> Community posts and third-party apps regularly point readers to new or existing TRW pages, indicating ongoing maintenance and updates.<ref>{{cite web |title=Tenrikyo Resource – Instagram post |url=https://www.instagram.com/p/DInqyBTRCz1/ |website=Instagram |date=2025 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Anecdotes Browser |url=https://lewdev.github.io/apps/anecdotes-browser/ |website=Lewdev |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==See also== *[[Tenrikyo]] *[[Tenrikyo Church Headquarters]] *[[Miki Nakayama]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[http://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/ Tenrikyo Resource Wiki] (official)
[[Category:Wikis]]

==See also== *[[Tenrikyo]] *[[Tenrikyo Church Headquarters]] *[[Miki Nakayama]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[http://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/ Tenrikyo Resource Wiki] (official)
[[Category:Wikis]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Online wiki about Tenrikyo}} {{Infobox website | name = '''Tenrikyo Resource Wiki''' | logo = | screenshot = | caption = | url = http://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/ | type = Online encyclopedia | language = English | owner = Lewis Nakao | launch date = active since 2011–2012 | current status = Online }}
The '''Tenrikyo Resource Wiki''' (often abbreviated '''TRW''') is a community-edited online encyclopedia focusing on the Japanese religion [[Tenrikyo]]. It describes itself as an initiative “to centralize all information on Tenrikyo and its teachings” and to serve as a one-stop reference for doctrine, history, scriptures, and practice.<ref>{{cite web |title=TRW:About |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/TRW%3AAbout |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/ |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> Independent sites and blogs describe TRW as an unofficial resource maintained by volunteers associated with the “Tenrikyo Resource” project.<ref>{{cite web |title=What is Tenrikyo? |url=https://thedailyofudesaki.wordpress.com/what-is-tenrikyo/ |website=The Daily Ofudesaki |date=2017 |quote=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki (unofficial). |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Links |url=https://tenrikyology.com/links/ |website=Tenrikyology |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==History== Evidence of the wiki’s activity dates to 2011–2012. A Wikimedia Commons file uploaded in September 2011 cites a TRW file page as its source, indicating the English-language subdomain was already operating,<ref>{{cite web |title=File:Tenrikyo Kanrodai sketch.jpg |url=https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tenrikyo_Kanrodai_sketch.jpg |website=Wikimedia Commons |date=September 22, 2011 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> and a 2012 “Weekly Wiki” blog post highlighted TRW pages to introduce Tenrikyo topics to readers.<ref>{{cite web |title=What is Tenrikyo? – Weekly Wiki |url=https://tenrikyo-resource.com/2448/what-is-tenrikyo-weekly-wiki?wptouch_switch=mobile |website=Tenrikyo Resource |date=October 12, 2012 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==Content and features== TRW hosts articles on core Tenrikyo concepts, scriptures, and practices, including entries on the ''[[Ofudesaki]]'' (“Tip of the Writing Brush”), the [[Mikagura-uta|''Kagura'' Service]] (''Tsutome''), doctrinal terms such as “Truth of Origin” (''moto no ri''), and roles such as ''yoboku'' (missionary).<ref>{{cite web |title=Ofudesaki |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Ofudesaki |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Kagura Service |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Kagura_Service |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Truth of Origin |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Truth_of_Origin |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Yoboku |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Yoboku |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> Additional sections include a downloads page for reference materials, and a glossary of Tenrikyo terms.<ref>{{cite web |title=Downloads |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Downloads |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Glossary Online |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Glossary_Online |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==See also== *[[Tenrikyo]] *[[Tenrikyo Church Headquarters]] *[[Miki Nakayama]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[http://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/ Tenrikyo Resource Wiki] (official)
[[Category:Wikis]] [[Category:Tenrikyo]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]]

It describes itself as an initiative “to centralize all information on Tenrikyo and its teachings” and to serve as a one-stop reference for doctrine, history, scriptures, and practice.<ref>{{cite web |title=TRW:About |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/TRW%3AAbout |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/ |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> Independent sites and blogs describe TRW as an unofficial resource maintained by volunteers associated with the “Tenrikyo Resource” project.<ref>{{cite web |title=What is Tenrikyo? |url=https://thedailyofudesaki.wordpress.com/what-is-tenrikyo/ |website=The Daily Ofudesaki |date=2017 |quote=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki (unofficial). |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Links |url=https://tenrikyology.com/links/ |website=Tenrikyology |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>

A Wikimedia Commons file uploaded in September 2011 cites a TRW file page as its source, indicating the English-language subdomain was already operating,<ref>{{cite web |title=File:Tenrikyo Kanrodai sketch.jpg |url=https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tenrikyo_Kanrodai_sketch.jpg |website=Wikimedia Commons |date=September 22, 2011 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> and a 2012 “Weekly Wiki” blog post highlighted TRW pages to introduce Tenrikyo topics to readers.<ref>{{cite web |title=What is Tenrikyo? – Weekly Wiki |url=https://tenrikyo-resource.com/2448/what-is-tenrikyo-weekly-wiki?wptouch_switch=mobile |website=Tenrikyo Resource |date=October 12, 2012 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==Content and features== TRW hosts articles on core Tenrikyo concepts, scriptures, and practices, including entries on the ''[[Ofudesaki]]'' (“Tip of the Writing Brush”), the [[Mikagura-uta|''Kagura'' Service]] (''Tsutome''), doctrinal terms such as “Truth of Origin” (''moto no ri''), and roles such as ''yoboku'' (missionary).<ref>{{cite web |title=Ofudesaki |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Ofudesaki |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Kagura Service |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Kagura_Service |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Truth of Origin |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Truth_of_Origin |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Yoboku |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Yoboku |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> Additional sections include a downloads page for reference materials, and a glossary of Tenrikyo terms.<ref>{{cite web |title=Downloads |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Downloads |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Glossary Online |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Glossary_Online |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==See also== *[[Tenrikyo]] *[[Tenrikyo Church Headquarters]] *[[Miki Nakayama]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[http://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/ Tenrikyo Resource Wiki] (official)

==History== Evidence of the wiki’s activity dates to 2011–2012. A Wikimedia Commons file uploaded in September 2011 cites a TRW file page as its source, indicating the English-language subdomain was already operating,<ref>{{cite web |title=File:Tenrikyo Kanrodai sketch.jpg |url=https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tenrikyo_Kanrodai_sketch.jpg |website=Wikimedia Commons |date=September 22, 2011 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> and a 2012 “Weekly Wiki” blog post highlighted TRW pages to introduce Tenrikyo topics to readers.<ref>{{cite web |title=What is Tenrikyo? – Weekly Wiki |url=https://tenrikyo-resource.com/2448/what-is-tenrikyo-weekly-wiki?wptouch_switch=mobile |website=Tenrikyo Resource |date=October 12, 2012 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==Content and features== TRW hosts articles on core Tenrikyo concepts, scriptures, and practices, including entries on the ''[[Ofudesaki]]'' (“Tip of the Writing Brush”), the [[Mikagura-uta|''Kagura'' Service]] (''Tsutome''), doctrinal terms such as “Truth of Origin” (''moto no ri''), and roles such as ''yoboku'' (missionary).<ref>{{cite web |title=Ofudesaki |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Ofudesaki |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Kagura Service |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Kagura_Service |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Truth of Origin |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Truth_of_Origin |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Yoboku |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Yoboku |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> Additional sections include a downloads page for reference materials, and a glossary of Tenrikyo terms.<ref>{{cite web |title=Downloads |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Downloads |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Glossary Online |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Glossary_Online |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==See also== *[[Tenrikyo]] *[[Tenrikyo Church Headquarters]] *[[Miki Nakayama]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[http://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/ Tenrikyo Resource Wiki] (official)

{{Short description|Online wiki about Tenrikyo}} {{Infobox website | name = '''Tenrikyo Resource Wiki''' | logo = | screenshot = | caption = | url = http://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/ | type = Online encyclopedia | language = English | owner = Lewis Nakao | launch date = active since 2011–2012 | current status = Online }}
The '''Tenrikyo Resource Wiki''' (often abbreviated '''TRW''') is a community-edited online encyclopedia focusing on the Japanese religion [[Tenrikyo]]. It describes itself as an initiative “to centralize all information on Tenrikyo and its teachings” and to serve as a one-stop reference for doctrine, history, scriptures, and practice.<ref>{{cite web |title=TRW:About |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/TRW%3AAbout |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/ |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> Independent sites and blogs describe TRW as an unofficial resource maintained by volunteers associated with the “Tenrikyo Resource” project.<ref>{{cite web |title=What is Tenrikyo? |url=https://thedailyofudesaki.wordpress.com/what-is-tenrikyo/ |website=The Daily Ofudesaki |date=2017 |quote=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki (unofficial). |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Links |url=https://tenrikyology.com/links/ |website=Tenrikyology |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==History== Evidence of the wiki’s activity dates to 2011–2012. A Wikimedia Commons file uploaded in September 2011 cites a TRW file page as its source, indicating the English-language subdomain was already operating,<ref>{{cite web |title=File:Tenrikyo Kanrodai sketch.jpg |url=https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tenrikyo_Kanrodai_sketch.jpg |website=Wikimedia Commons |date=September 22, 2011 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> and a 2012 “Weekly Wiki” blog post highlighted TRW pages to introduce Tenrikyo topics to readers.<ref>{{cite web |title=What is Tenrikyo? – Weekly Wiki |url=https://tenrikyo-resource.com/2448/what-is-tenrikyo-weekly-wiki?wptouch_switch=mobile |website=Tenrikyo Resource |date=October 12, 2012 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==Content and features== TRW hosts articles on core Tenrikyo concepts, scriptures, and practices, including entries on the ''[[Ofudesaki]]'' (“Tip of the Writing Brush”), the [[Mikagura-uta|''Kagura'' Service]] (''Tsutome''), doctrinal terms such as “Truth of Origin” (''moto no ri''), and roles such as ''yoboku'' (missionary).<ref>{{cite web |title=Ofudesaki |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Ofudesaki |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Kagura Service |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Kagura_Service |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Truth of Origin |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Truth_of_Origin |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Yoboku |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Yoboku |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> Additional sections include a downloads page for reference materials, and a glossary of Tenrikyo terms.<ref>{{cite web |title=Downloads |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Downloads |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Glossary Online |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Glossary_Online |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
